headline: School Dropout Rate in Sabah at 8.4% Triple the National Average
context: Sabah's primary and secondary school dropout rate of 8.4% far exceeds the national rate of 2.8% in 2025.

cards.big: Sabah school dropout rate at 8.4% is triple the national average of 2.8% in 2025
cards.sub: Poverty distance to school and documentation issues drive early school leaving

cards.big: 32,000 children in Sabah lack birth certificates preventing school enrollment
cards.sub: Stateless and undocumented children face legal barriers to education access

cards.big: Average distance to nearest secondary school in rural Sabah is 23km versus 4km nationally
cards.sub: Transportation barriers compound economic factors in dropout decisions

cards.big: Philippines' conditional cash transfer reduced dropout rates in remote areas by 38% since 2016
cards.sub: Direct financial support addresses economic root causes of school abandonment

cards.big: Sabah's dropout crisis is not about student motivation—it is about systemic access failure
Should Sabah receive differentiated education funding reflecting its unique access challenges
